images.alt: Home Medix HM-CV-20 auto CPAP machine — compact white chassis with integrated heated humidifier

faqs.q: What modes does the HM-CV-20 support?
faqs.a: CPAP (fixed-pressure) and APAP (auto-adjusting). Pressure range is 4–20 cmH₂O per the published technical specification.

faqs.q: How loud is the HM-CV-20 at bedside?
faqs.a: The published specification is <30 dB. That is at the quiet end of the CPAP class — below typical library-ambient noise (40 dB) and competitive with premium imports like the ResMed AirSense 11 (27 dB) and Philips DreamStation 2 (25 dB).
Yes. Heated humidifier with 0–5 adjustable levels is built in; the overall 1.45 kg weight is with the humidifier chamber attached. Heated-tube compatibility is not stated in the published spec.

EPFlex is Home Medix's expiratory pressure relief feature — the machine reduces pressure during exhalation to reduce the patient's sense of pressurised-exhale resistance. Functionally equivalent to ResMed's EPR or Philips' C-Flex.

description_text: The HM-CV-20 is Home Medix's auto CPAP, built around a patented SAF algorithm that combines advanced flow-sensing with leak-compensated auto-adjustment. Published specs: CPAP + APAP modes, 4–20 cmH₂O pressure range, <30 dB sound, 1.45 kg with integrated heated humidifier, 253 × 168 × 121 mm footprint. EPFlex expiratory pressure relief, multi-level respiratory event detection (flow limitation, snoring, hypopnea, and apnea including OSA and CSA), and ISO 9001 + ISO 13485 + CDSCO approval. Positioned against the ResMed AirSense, Philips DreamStation, and BMC GII auto CPAP tier.
